docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Codeblocks
docs.text:
Sample C code

#include <stdio.h>

int main() {
    printf("Hello, World!\n");
    return 0;
}

Codeblock with title and line numbers

test.c
#include <stdio.h>

int main() {
    printf("Hello, World!\n");
    return 0;
}

docs.title: llvm::StoreInst Class
docs.text:
The public member function Value* getPointerOperand () is a wrapper function for getOperand(1).

llvm/include/llvm/IR/Instructions.h
Value *getPointerOperand() { return getOperand(1); }

The first class types are perhaps the most important. Values of these types are the only ones which can be produced by instructions.

docs.title: Build
docs.text: Note: SVF uses CMake to generate build files for compiling the project. Starting with SVF version 2.9, the framework leverages the FILE_SET feature introduced in CMake version 3.23, which allows grouping related files within a target. Therefore, to successfully generate build files for SVF version 3, you must use CMake version 3.23 or higher.
